name: Fresh Mint Ice Cream with Chocolate Sauce
recipeInstructions:
For Ice Cream: Combine the milk and chopped mint in a heavy medium saucepan. Bring to a simmer. Remove from heat, cover, and let stand for 30 minutes. Mix in the cream. Using an electric mixer, beat the egg yolks, sugar, and salt in a large bowl until pale yellow and forming a slowly dissolving ribbon when the beaters are lifted (about 3 minutes). Gradually beat in the milk mixture. Return the mixture to the saucepan and stir over medium-low heat until the custard thickens and leaves a path on the back of a spoon when your finger is drawn across it (do not boil). Cool, strain through a sieve into a clean bowl, cover, and chill. Process the custard in an ice cream maker according to the manufacturer's instructions. Transfer to a covered container and freeze. This can be prepared up to 3 days ahead.
For Sauce: Melt the butter in a heavy small saucepan over low heat. Whisk in the cream, sugar, cocoa powder (sifted), and salt, and simmer until the sauce thickens slightly (whisking frequently) for about 5 minutes. This can be prepared up to 3 days ahead. Cover and refrigerate. Rewarm over low heat before serving. Scoop ice cream into bowls and spoon the warm sauce over.
